description: Trigger discrimination for designing the SAST RUN (this skill) vs the finding-triage JUDGMENT it yields to (static-analysis-reviewer), the whole-repo multi-tool aggregation it feeds (security-scan-orchestrator, in-batch pinned both ways), the dependency/SCA category it is not (supply-chain-security-reviewer), and the delivery pipeline it produces a stage contract for (ci-pipeline-architect).

cases.prompt: Design how our SAST should run over this repo: which analyzer category per language, the ruleset and severity thresholds versioned in-repo, a baseline plus diff-scanning that gates only new findings on a PR with a full scan nightly, a governed suppression list, and where it sits in CI with a fail-closed rule.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — analyzer selection, ruleset/config, baseline+diff, governed suppression, incremental-vs-full, and fail-closed CI integration ARE this skill's parts. No finding interpretation, no whole-repo multi-tool aggregation is requested.
cases.reason: The subject is the design of the SAST RUN itself. It yields finding interpretation to static-analysis-reviewer and feeds one input to security-scan-orchestrator's aggregate; it is neither of those.

cases.prompt: Go through this SAST output and decide which findings are real, rank the true positives by exploitability, and record the rationale for the ones we're calling false positives.
cases.note: ORCHESTRATE-vs-TRIAGE SEAM (mandatory) — deciding TP/FP, ranking, and writing the suppression VERDICT is the triage judgment. This skill designs the run that produces findings; static-analysis-reviewer judges them and owns the verdict the governed list records.
cases.reason: Interpreting findings and writing suppression verdicts is static-analysis-reviewer. sast-orchestration-designer designs the governed suppression LIST and the run; it does not decide which findings are false positives.

cases.prompt: Coordinate all our scanners over the whole repo — SAST, the dependency scanner, the secret scanner, the infra-config scanner — and give me one aggregated, deduplicated report with a coverage and gap list.
cases.note: IN-BATCH SEAM (pinned both ways) — coordinating the whole multi-category suite and aggregating into one report is the orchestrator's job. This skill designs the SAST category's run, which is one input to that aggregate.
cases.reason: Whole-repo multi-tool aggregation is security-scan-orchestrator. sast-orchestration-designer designs only the SAST run and hands its normalized output to that aggregator.

cases.prompt: We need to scan our dependencies and lockfile for vulnerable and typosquatted packages and check the install scripts.
cases.reason: Dependency/SCA scanning and provenance judgment is supply-chain-security-reviewer. sast-orchestration-designer is first-party-code static analysis (SAST), a different category from dependency scanning.

cases.prompt: Design our whole CI pipeline — the stage graph from lint through deploy, merge-blocking semantics, secret governance, and rollback primitives.
cases.reason: Designing the delivery pipeline is ci-pipeline-architect. sast-orchestration-designer produces the SAST stage's contract (placement, merge-blocking, fail-closed rule) that plugs into that pipeline; it does not design the pipeline.
